studyName: Systemic Inflammation and Lymphocyte Activation Precede Rheumatoid Arthritis

tables.description: This sample attributes data table includes each sample's body site of origin, analyte type, tumor status, hashtag sequence, and number of subject's days prior to a diagnosis of rheumatoid arthritis.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: DAYS_TO_RA_DIAGNOSIS
variables.description: Number of days to conversion to rheumatoid arthritis

variables.name: DUPLICATED_SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: SAMPLE_ID of sample used in the overlapping dbGaP study

variables.name: DUPLICATE_SAMPLE
variables.description: Sequence data for duplicate samples are available through data access request to the overlapping dbGaP study whose accession number is indicated

variables.name: HASHTAG_SEQUENCE
variables.description: The sequence of hash oligo for sample

tables.description: This subject consent data table includes subject IDs, consent information, biological sex, and aliases to subject IDs used in other dbGaP studies.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Registered consent groups (Data Use Limitations (DUL)) as determined by submitters' Institutional Review Boards (IRB) or equivalent body.

variables.name: SOURCE_SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID used in the Source Repository

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table reports each subject's race, ethnicity, disease status based on anti-citrullinated protein autoantibody (ACPA) levels, and rheumatoid arthritis conversion status.

variables.name: CONVERTER STATUS
variables.description: Status of conversion to rheumatoid arthritis (RA)

variables.name: GROUP
variables.description: Disease status based on anti-citrullinated protein autoantibody (ACPA) levels

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to individual  and pooled sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
This data table maps the sample IDs that appear in the subject sample mapping dataset file to the corresponding accession numbrs of the samples in NCBI's Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) database.
